overview: This Mono-Black Pauper deck utilizes cheap, aggressive creatures and board-wide pressure to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. Defile provides constant life drain and creature attrition, while Troll of Khazad-dum offers efficient early threats. Cast Down ensures you can bypass larger blockers to close out games quickly.
win_conditions: Combat damage from early aggressive creatures
Defile upkeep life loss combined with board presence
Direct damage from Troll of Khazad-dum triggers

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one- or two-drops
Hands containing Defile or Troll of Khazad-dum
Hands with early removal like Cast Down
Keep hands with 1-2 lands and at least one interaction (Defile or Cast Down) or Troll of Khazad-dum.
Drop hands with 3+ lands, no removal, or only high-cost threats.
Prioritize hands that can play a land and a removal spell or a 2-drop threat by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only three-drops or higher
Hands with multiple removal spells but no creatures

early_game: Play cheap creatures immediately to establish board presence
Cast Defile as soon as possible to start life drain and weaken opposing creatures
Use Cast Down to remove early blockers or finish the opponent
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally hold removal for turn 2.
Turn 2: Develop board with Troll or use Defile/Cast Down on opponent's key creature.
Sequence lands to ensure black mana availability for spot removal on turn 2 or 3.
if facing control/combo, develop Troll to apply pressure and prepare for graveyard/token synergy.
mid_game: Maintain pressure with additional Troll of Khazad-dum copies
Trade efficiently to keep Defile's damage output relevant
Prioritize removal for threats that can stall your aggression
Preserve removal for high-value targets like finishers or combo pieces.
Use Troll's death trigger to generate board presence when forced to trade.
Manage hand size by trading removal for tempo or threats, and avoid overcommitting mana when opponent is low on resources.
Commit threats when you have removal to handle counters or sweepers.
Hold back removal if opponent is out of resources or if you can out-value them with Troll's tokens.
Prioritize trading Troll for their best creature to maximize value.
late_game: Push for lethal with remaining creatures and Defile damage
Use Cast Down to clear the path for a final combat
Accept trades if you are ahead on life total and board state
Troll of Khazad-dum (and its tokens) combined with spot removal to clear the path.
Cast Down/Defile to remove blockers or finish low-life opponents.
When you have board control, opponent is at low life, or you can trade removal for their win condition.
Commit when you can force favorable trades with Troll's death trigger.

key_cards.name: Defile
key_cards.notes: Play early to maximize upkeep damage and weaken opposing creatures over time. Protect it when possible but be willing to trade it for board control.

key_cards.name: Troll of Khazad-dum
key_cards.role: Early aggressive threat and spot removal
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn one or two to apply immediate pressure. Use its enter-the-battlefield damage to kill small creatures or chip away at the opponent's life total.

key_cards.name: Cast Down
key_cards.notes: Hold for the opponent's turn to maximize its impact. Use it to clear blockers before attacking or to finish off an opponent who is low on life.

sequencing_tips: Cast Defile before playing creatures to immediately weaken the board
Save Cast Down for the opponent's turn to maximize its impact
Use Troll of Khazad-dum's trigger to remove blockers before attacking with it
Avoid overcommitting to board wipes if you lack follow-up.
Troll's death trigger is a key resource—don't sacrifice it unnecessarily.
Track opponent's mana and hand size to predict removal usage.
In the Unknown format, adapt to specific metagame shifts by prioritizing hate cards against top-tier archetypes, and always keep a backup plan if your primary game plan is disrupted.

matchups.advice: Prioritize keeping Defile to kill their small creatures and buy time. Use Cast Down on their biggest threats to maintain board control.
Defile is highly effective against their low-toughness creatures. Use Cast Down to remove larger threats that can block your Trolls.
Apply maximum early pressure before they stabilize. Use Troll of Khazad-dum to chip away at their life total and force them to use removal inefficiently.

Use removal on threats before they can be countered, or hold removal for when they blow mana on counters. Trade Troll for their uncounterable threats and apply pressure with tokens to force suboptimal counter decisions.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use spot removal on key ramp creatures. Troll's tokens can outpace their early game, and you can stabilize by removing their mana sources.
Stabilize with removal on turn 2/3 threats. Use Troll to generate tokens and stabilize. Prioritize life gain if available, and avoid overcommitting to board wipes without follow-up.

Apply consistent pressure with Troll. Use removal to clear their blockers. Out-value them with card advantage and resilient threats, forcing them to spend resources on interaction rather than their game plan.

Value trades are key. Use Troll to force unfavorable trades. Save removal for their threats. Prioritize hand disruption if present, and track their graveyard to avoid falling for reanimation or recursion.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counters, or combo win conditions.
Note when opponents save removal vs. when they commit it.
Track graveyard usage if they play discard or reanimation, and monitor their mana to predict removal windows.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Defile, Troll of Khazad-dum, Cast Down

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Defile, Cast Down, Troll of Khazad-dum
